name: skill
description: Load a skill's full instructions by name. The available skills are listed in the <skills> block of the system prompt (name + one-line description). Call this with a skill's name to get its complete SKILL.md instructions, then follow them. This is the direct way to invoke a skill — you do not need to know or read its file path. A skill whose frontmatter declares `context: fork` runs in a one-shot sub-agent instead (when dynamic sub-agents are enabled): the call becomes a spawn and you receive a handle, with the result arriving later as a sub-agent report — pass `task` to tell the forked run what to do.

name.description: The skill name exactly as it appears in the <skills> directory block.

task.description: For context:fork skills only: the concrete task for the forked one-shot run (what to apply the skill to). Ignored for inline skills.
